title: Resistance Exercise and Muscle Protein Synthesis in Middle-Aged Men
author: Smith, J. & Johnson, M.
ai_reason: Directly relevant to muscle hypertrophy in aging men
ai_summary: Study demonstrates that high-intensity resistance training combined with adequate protein intake increases muscle protein synthesis in men aged 50-65, with implications for preventing age-related muscle loss.

title: Long-term Effects of Strength Training on Lean Mass Maintenance
author: Chen, L. et al.
ai_reason: Addresses sarcopenia prevention in middle-aged population
ai_summary: Longitudinal study showing sustained lean mass gains in men aged 45-70 who maintain consistent resistance exercise programs over 2 years.

title: Hormonal Response to Progressive Overload Training in Aging Athletes
author: Williams, R.
ai_reason: Examines anabolic response in aging population
ai_summary: Research on testosterone and IGF-1 responses to progressive strength training in men over 50, showing maintained anabolic signaling with proper training protocols.

title: Nutritional Strategies for Optimizing Muscle Hypertrophy in Older Adults
author: Thompson, D. & Brown, K.
ai_reason: Practical nutrition guidance for age-appropriate muscle building
ai_summary: Review of protein timing, amino acid distribution, and micronutrient requirements for maximizing muscle adaptation to resistance training in middle-aged and older men.

title: Recovery Optimization for High-Frequency Resistance Training in Middle-Age
author: Garcia, M. et al.
ai_reason: Addresses recovery strategies for middle-aged lifters
ai_summary: Investigation of sleep, deload protocols, and active recovery methods for men aged 45-60 performing high-frequency strength training without overtraining.
